assertions.evidence:
@TableTest("""
        Scenario       | Input | Amount?
        Whole dollars  | 10.00 | 10.00
        Minimum value  | 0.01  | 0.01
        Zero           | 0.00  | 0.00
        """)

assertions.text: Null input is represented as a blank cell (not the string 'null') in the table
The exception cases (empty string, letters-only, negative) are handled — via a Throws? column, assertThrows in the method body, or a separate @TableTest — not silently omitted
Valid inputs have a result column name ending with '?' (e.g. 'Money?' or 'Result?')
Test method body contains no if or switch statements
assertions.evidence: No if or switch keywords appear in any of the three method bodies (validInput_returnsMoneyWithAmount, nullInput_returnsNull, invalidInput_throwsIllegalArgumentException). All method bodies contain only direct assertions and lambda expressions.
